task: A friend asks you to cover a small errand this week, but your schedule is already packed. What’s a straightforward way to decide whether you can take it on, and how would you answer so the other person knows where things stand?

turns.agent_response:
Use a quick go/no-go check:

- Do I have real open time, not just “maybe” time?
- Will this push out something already locked in?
- Can I do it without creating a chain reaction in my week?
- If I say yes, can I actually follow through?

If any of those are shaky, it’s a no.

A straightforward answer would be:

- “I can’t take that on this week.”
- “I’m already packed, so I need to pass.”
- “I’m not able to commit to that right now.”

If you want, I can also give you a few versions that sound more casual, firmer, or more polite.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically it. I’d just check if I’ve got actual open time, not fantasy time, and whether saying yes screws up something else. If it does, it’s a no.

I’d answer plain:
- “Can’t take that on this week.”
- “I’m booked up, so I have to pass.”
- “Not able to commit to it right now.”

Clean. No extra story. People hear the line and know where they stand.
